title: Task Processing Pipeline (GeneraticAgent.run)
activity_flow: START: Caller invokes put_task(query, source, images)
put_task enqueues {query, source, images, output:display_queue} onto task_queue
LOOP: task_queue.get() — blocks until task available
Unpack task: raw_query, source, images, display_queue
YES: set llmclient.backend.<k>=v → display_queue.put({'done':..., 'source':'system'}) → return None
YES: return injected resume prompt string
YES: task_queue.task_done() → continue (skip to next task)
[BRANCH] Previous handler exists and has key_info?
YES: carry over key_info and increment passed_sessions counter into new handler
Invoke agent_runner_loop(llmclient, sys_prompt, user_input, handler, TOOLS_SCHEMA, max_turns=40) → generator
LOOP: for chunk in agent_runner_loop generator:
[CHECK] stop_sig == True? → break out of chunk loop
[BRANCH] buffer grown > 50 chars OR chunk contains 'LLM Running'?
YES: display_queue.put({'next': incremental_or_full, 'source': source}); update last_pos
Post-process full_resp: fix </summary> newlines, reformat <file_content> blocks
[BRANCH] stop_sig True? → log 'User aborted the task.'
handler.code_stop_signal.append(1) — signal any running sub-process to stop
agent_runner_loop_internal: For each tool_call: handler.dispatch(tool_name, args) → StepOutcome
handler.turn_end_callback(response, tool_calls, tool_results, turn, next_prompt, exit_reason)

key_data_structures.task_item: {query: str, source: str, images: list, output: queue.Queue}
key_data_structures.display_queue_messages: {'next': str, 'source': str} for streaming; {'done': str, 'source': str} for completion/error

key_conditions: /session.<k>=<v>: sets LLM backend attribute, returns None (skip LLM call)
/resume: returns a canned prompt for context recovery
stop_sig: checked every chunk iteration, triggers abort
inc_out flag: controls whether display_queue gets incremental deltas or full accumulation
source == 'feishu': injects anchor/context prompt for Feishu messages with history
chunk buffer threshold: 50 chars or 'LLM Running' string triggers intermediate push
important_notes: GeneraticAgent.run() is a blocking infinite loop, always run in a daemon thread. put_task() is the sole entry point; it returns a display_queue the caller polls. agent_runner_loop is a Python generator (yield-based streaming). Slash commands that return None short-circuit the LLM call entirely. Handler (GenericAgentHandler) carries key_info working memory across sessions. The finally block always marks task_done and kills sub-process signals.
